response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with nearly two decades of broad software experience and a genuine open-source track record (founder of an OSS geo-data platform, D-language libraries, FOSS4G speaker), which aligns well with the open-source and Linux orientation of the role. However, the role is an executive-level leadership position requiring German at C1 minimum, and the resume explicitly lists German as Basic — a hard disqualifier for client-facing consulting work in Germany. He also shows no formal team-leadership or people-management experience, no stated UML/requirements-management practice, no Python, and no demonstrated IT-security, risk, or compliance background.
strengths: Deep, genuine open-source engagement: OSS platform founder, D-language libraries, FOSS4G speaker, Linux experience
Extensive (19+ years) full-stack breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing through workshops, courses, and community teaching
concerns: German listed as Basic; the role requires minimum C1 for a client-advisory leadership position
No formal team-lead or engineering-management experience for an executive-level role
Missing several stated requirements: UML/modeling, Python, requirements management, and IT security/risk/compliance experience
